notes: Crude-grade assignments for the 39 Group-A refineries. Sources: 'company_disclosure' (operator/IR fact sheet), 'industry_report' (RBN/S&P/Argus/Reuters/wiki), 'inferred_from_config' (NCI + coker + preferred_slate seed), 'inferred_operator_region' (operator profile + PADD/duoarea + pipeline access), 'inferred_plant_type' (asphalt/lubricant/specialty). The 23-grade vocabulary contains no foreign crudes; refineries with material foreign (WCS/Maya/Merey) reliance are flagged in the notes. Loader persists only refinery_id, commodity, is_primary, source, notes.

grades.notes: Largest US refinery; Saudi Aramco affiliate runs Arab Medium/Heavy imports as the true anchor, with Mars the closest US medium-sour analog moved via Gulf Coast pipelines.
Standard offshore-medium-sour blend reaching Port Arthur via Texas Gulf pipelines.
Heavier Gulf offshore sour used to push the slate heavier when import availability slips.
Available via Gulf Coast offshore systems; complements Mars.
Light-sweet diluent for blending; Houston access via TX Gulf hub.
Louisiana Light Sweet via interconnects; minor share.
Generic placeholder for imported Arab Medium/Heavy (Aramco's natural feedstock) which is outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
597 kbd LA Gulf Coast heavy-sour anchor; NCI 12.5 + coker + hydrocracker. Mars is the workhorse medium-sour.
Standard LA offshore medium-sour.
Heavy Gulf offshore sour suited to the deep-conversion configuration.
Lighter offshore sour; LOOP/CHOPS access.
Louisiana Light Sweet via LOCAP.
Light sweet via Capline reversal / TX Gulf interconnects.
WCS / Mayan imports are a meaningful share of the actual slate but are not in the 23-grade vocabulary.
593 kbd TX Gulf Coast medium-sour preferred-slate; NCI 12 + coker + hydrocracker.
Standard Texas-City offshore medium-sour blend.
Available via Gulf offshore pipelines.
Heavy Gulf sour fits the coker.
Light-sweet blending stream from the Houston hub.
Permian sweet via Longhorn/Cactus to Texas City.
Short-haul South Texas sweet.
ExxonMobil flagship TX Gulf Coast medium-sour; NCI 13 + coker + hydrocracker.
Standard Gulf Coast offshore medium-sour.
ExxonMobil-equity offshore sour; natural Baytown feedstock.
Heavy Gulf offshore sour.
Light-sweet blending stream from Houston hub.
Permian sweet via long-haul pipelines.
South Texas sweet via gathering systems.
Major PADD2 inland refinery; Bakken via Enbridge Mainline & Express provides the light-sweet portion of the slate.
WTI Cushing barrels reach Whiting via the Enbridge/Mid-Valley/ETCO system.
Wyoming heavy sour suits the coker; reachable via Express/Platte.
Rocky Mountain sweet via Express/Platte.
WCS is Whiting's true heavy-sour anchor (~ 60-70% of slate post-2013 reconfiguration) but is not in the 23-grade vocabulary; recorded here as generic crude.
Post-2019 PDVSA-sanctions slate leans heavily on Gulf-offshore mediums; Mars is the primary US analog after Merey was lost.
Heavy Gulf offshore sour suits the deep-coker (one of the highest coking capacities on the Gulf Coast).
Offshore medium-sour blending stream.
Lighter offshore sour.
Louisiana Light Sweet for blending.
Historically designed around Venezuelan Merey/Boscan; with sanctions, replacement barrels are Iraqi Basrah Heavy and Colombian Castilla, neither in the 23-grade vocabulary.
Integrated Carson + Wilmington complex; California heavy crude is the natural local feedstock.
San Joaquin Valley heavy from Kern reaches LA Basin via SJV pipelines.
Alaska North Slope is the dominant waterborne medium-sour at LA refineries.
Asphaltic California crude; suits the coker.
Marine-delivered Permian sweet for blending (NCI 13, hydrocracker).
Waterborne light-sweet via Pacific tanker.
PADD2 heavy/sweet swing refinery; Bakken via Enbridge.
WTI Cushing via the Borger / Wood River system.
Rocky Mountain heavy-sour suits the coker.
Via Platte/Pony Express pipelines.
WCS is the dominant heavy-sour anchor at the WRB JV (Cenovus equity barrels) but is not in the 23-grade vocabulary.
PADD2 high-complexity refinery; Bakken via Enbridge/Mid-Valley to Patoka.
WTI Cushing via Mid-Valley/Capline reversal.
Gulf Coast medium-sour via reversed Capline; suits NCI 13 + coker + hydrocracker.
Louisiana Light Sweet via Capline reversal.
WCS-equivalent Canadian heavy is a meaningful share but is not in the 23-grade vocabulary.
PADD2 lower-complexity refinery; Bakken via Enbridge is the light-sweet workhorse.
WTI Cushing via Pony Express / Diamond pipelines.
Rocky Mountain sweet via Platte/Mid-Valley.
WCS/Cold Lake Canadian heavy is the dominant slate (Joliet was reconfigured around Athabasca synbit) but is outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
East Coast refinery; Bakken-by-rail is the primary domestic feedstock since the shale boom.
Jones-Act tanker movements of Eagle Ford light to NY/NJ.
Jones-Act tanker WTI Houston for blending.
Coker enables limited medium-sour runs; Mars is the most-accessible US sour by tanker.
Imported West African (Bonny Light) and North Sea grades are material but outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
PADD5 (Ferndale, WA); ANS is the workhorse medium-sour by tanker from Valdez.
Bakken-by-rail terminal at Cherry Point provides light-sweet optionality.
Rocky Mountain sweet via rail.
Coker-supported heavy California crude via Pacific tanker.
Canadian heavy via Trans Mountain pipeline (Cold Lake/AWB) is a major slate component; not in the 23-grade vocabulary.
Bay Area refinery; ANS by tanker is the primary medium-sour.
San Joaquin Valley heavy via SJV pipelines.
Kern County heavy via SJV pipelines.
Asphaltic California crude; hydrocracker enables conversion.
Waterborne or rail light-sweet for blending.
Tanker-delivered Permian for blending.
Ponca City sits ~ 60 miles from Cushing; WTI Cushing is the primary feedstock.
Local Oklahoma sweet via gathering systems.
Permian sweet via Basin/Centurion pipelines into Cushing.
Bakken via Pony Express into Cushing.
Niobrara/Wyoming via Pony Express.
DJ Basin sweet into Cushing.
NCI 13 + coker + hydrocracker supports some medium-sour; via Seaway reversal from Gulf.
East Coast; PBF's Delaware City was the original Bakken-by-rail receiver.
Jones-Act tanker Eagle Ford to East Coast.
NCI 11.5 + coker + hydrocracker supports medium-sour; Mars via Jones-Act tanker.
Light-sweet tanker movements from Houston.
Delaware City runs significant imported heavy-sour (Mayan/Castilla/Iraqi) via rail and marine; outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
SLC cluster runs Uinta/Wyoming-origin sweets via local pipelines and trucks.
DJ Basin / Niobrara sweet via Frontier/Tallgrass.
Bakken via Belle Fourche/Butte pipelines.
Low-NCI cluster (~ 9), so light-sweet dominates.

PADD2 inland; Bakken via Enbridge Mainline.
WTI Cushing via Mid-Valley.

Wyoming-origin sweets via local pipelines.
Bakken via Pony Express.
Asphalt-focused product slate uses Wyoming heavy / Niobrara asphaltic blends.
Suncor-equity Canadian SCO/WCS is material here but outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
Wyoming-local sweet via Sinclair gathering.
Niobrara via Pony Express / Saddlehorn.
Medium-sour preferred slate at NCI 10 supports asphaltic Wyoming heavy.
Bakken via Belle Fourche.
Canadian heavy via Express provides residual heavy-sour capacity.
Petromax Houston is a topping / condensate-splitter complex; Eagle Ford condensate is the natural feedstock.
Eagle Ford light sweet via Houston gathering.
Light WTI from Houston hub.
Permian condensate via long-haul pipelines.
West Texas Light fits topping-unit kit.
Phillips 66 Lake Charles (Westlake) is fed via the Clifton Ridge / Bayou Bridge systems; LLS is a natural anchor.
Refinery is rated for sweet, medium sour, heavy sour, and high-acid crude (NCI ~ 9.5 with coker, hydrocracker); Mars is the local medium-sour.
Standard LA Gulf offshore medium-sour.
Heavy Gulf offshore sour; suits the delayed coker.
Offshore sour via Gulf systems.
Light-sweet WTI via Houston interconnect.
Billings runs domestic high-sulphur and low-sulphur crude via pipeline; Wyoming-origin sweet is a natural fit.
Domestic high-sulphur Wyoming heavy / asphaltic.
DJ Basin Niobrara via Pony Express.
Operator stated Bakken is unlikely on economics but is technically processable.
Canadian heavy sulphur (Bow River / WCS) via Express pipeline is the dominant feedstock; up to 100% heavy-Canadian capable. Outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
Sweeny is a TX Gulf Coast complex with crude-switching capability (~ 40 kbd swing between heavy and light); WTI Houston is the primary light.
Kinder Morgan Eagle Ford pipeline supplies ~ 30 kbd.
Permian sweet via Houston hub.
Heavy-side swing supplies Gulf medium-sour.
Standard Gulf offshore medium-sour.

Light sweet via interconnects.
Mississippi-river barge of Eagle Ford.
Up to 2.0 wt% sulphur capability; Mars fits the medium-sour upper bound.
LA Gulf medium-sour.
United Refining reconfigured around asphalt-based crude; Wyoming asphaltic is the closest US analog.
Bakken-by-rail/pipeline for light-sweet balance.
Rocky Mountain sweet via long-haul pipelines.
Substantially all Canadian asphaltic crude via the Enbridge system to West Seneca; outside the 23-grade vocabulary.
San Joaquin Refining processes only San Joaquin Valley heavy naphthenic crude (Kern County leases).
California heavy is the umbrella feedstock category.
Midway-Sunset is a core Kern naphthenic stream feeding the speciality naphthenic-oil product line.
Shell-operated, ~ 230 kbd LA Gulf Coast refinery with RCCU + coker + hydrocracker; Mars is the LA medium-sour anchor.
Standard LA Gulf offshore medium-sour.
Shell-equity offshore sour; natural Norco feedstock.
Heavy Gulf offshore sour for the coker.
Louisiana Light Sweet via LOOP.
Light-sweet WTI via TX-LA pipelines.
Silver Eagle Evanston is fed by condensates via pipeline and truck; Wyoming-origin condensate / sweet is the natural feedstock.

Topping unit accepts condensates; included for completeness.
San Antonio refinery (Starlight, formerly Calumet/NuStar) is permitted for South Texas condensate and light sweet, 40-53 API.
Currently most crude is Eagle Ford via TexStar pipeline.
Permian light via interconnects.
West Texas Light fits the design API.
Talley is a small Kern County asphalt plant; Kern heavy is the only realistic feedstock.
Asphaltic Midway-Sunset stream suits asphalt production.

Texas International Terminals Galveston is a 50 kbd topping unit fed by a 24-inch pipeline from Texas City; WTI Houston is the dominant feedstock.

Valero McKee (Sunray) has access to Permian Basin crude via third-party pipelines; ~ 40% of crude is from the McKee crude system.
